definition: a customer of a store or supporter of a group
example: The library patron checked out seven books and promised to return them on time.

definition: the highest point of a mountain or the highest level
example: The climbers reached the peak of Mount Everest just as the sun was rising.

definition: a large farm where one crop is grown in huge quantities
example: The tea plantation covered the entire hillside with rows of bright green bushes.

definition: to ask for something with great emotion and urgency
example: The lost puppy seemed to plead with its big brown eyes for someone to take it home.

definition: a survey asking people their opinions or who they'll vote for
example: The class took a poll to decide whether to have a pizza party, or a movie day.

definition: to transfer pollen to a plant so it can make seeds
example: Bees pollinate flowers as they fly from bloom to bloom collecting nectar.

definition: to make air, water, or land dirty and harmful
example: Factories that dump chemicals into rivers pollute the water and harm fish.

definition: to fill an area with people or living things
example: Over time, settlers began to populate the frontier and build new towns.

definition: an action taken ahead of time to prevent something bad
example: Wearing sunscreen is a precaution that protects your skin from painful sunburn.

definition: coming before the main event as getting ready
example: The preliminary rounds narrowed the field before the championship race.

definition: a statement or idea that forms the basis for an argument
example: The premise of the story is that animals can talk when humans aren't watching.

definition: to be in charge of a meeting or ceremony
example: The vice president will preside over the Senate meeting when it begins at noon.

definition: common and widespread in a particular place or time
example: Allergies are especially prevalent in spring when trees and flowers release pollen.

definition: especially; more than anything else
example: Pandas eat primarily bamboo—it makes up ninety-nine percent of their diet.

definition: a set of steps followed in a specific order to do something
example: The fire drill procedure tells students to line up quietly and walk to the exit.

definition: a fixed amount or share that is expected or allowed
example: Each student had a quota of five books to read before the end of the semester.

definition: based on reason and clear thinking rather than emotions
example: Taking a rain cover when clouds appear is a rational choice.

definition: what you do or feel in response to something that happens
example: Her reaction to the surprise party was to scream with joy and hug everyone.

definition: to move back or become smaller over time
example: After the storm, the floodwaters began to recede, and the streets became visible again.

definition: a formal party to welcome people; also, the way people react to something
example: The author's new book received a warm reception—fans lined up for hours to buy it.

definition: to direct someone to a source of facts or mention something
example: The teacher told us to refer to chapter four for the answers to tonight's homework.

definition: a safe place where you are protected from danger
example: The bird sanctuary provides refuge for injured eagles until they can fly again.

definition: a government or ruling system, especially one that uses strict control
example: The history book described how the old regime made strict rules that limited people's freedom.

definition: to pass a message along to someone else, or a team race where each runner takes a turn
example: She asked her brother to relay the message that dinner would be ready at six.

definition: to cause something to become a certain way, often in a big or lasting manner
example: The heavy snow rendered the roads too dangerous to drive on.

definition: to fix up and improve an old building
example: They plan to renovate the old theater by adding new seats, and a bigger stage.

definition: to officially cancel or take back a law
example: Citizens voted to repeal the old law that banned skateboarding in the park.

definition: to copy or reproduce something perfectly
example: Scientists are trying to replicate how the human brain learns inside a computer.

definition: to officially quit a job or position
example: The coach decided to resign after twenty years, so she could spend more time with family.

definition: to hold back or prevent from doing something
example: The dog owner had to restrain her excited puppy from jumping on the visitors.

definition: the selling of goods to customers in stores
example: The retail price of the jacket was sixty dollars, but he found it on sale for forty.

definition: to get something back or go and bring it
example: The golden retriever loves to retrieve tennis balls thrown into the lake.

definition: to move in a circle around a central point
example: Earth revolves around the sun once every 365 days, giving us our year.

definition: strong, healthy, and able to handle tough conditions
example: The robust oak tree survived the storm while thinner trees snapped in the wind.

definition: to turn around a center point like a wheel
example: Earth rotates on its axis once every twenty-four hours giving us day and night.

definition: official permission to do something, or a penalty placed on a country for breaking rules
example: After the country broke the trade agreement, other nations imposed sanctions to pressure it to change.

definition: an object that orbits a planet, either natural like the moon or human-made
example: GPS works by communicating with satellites orbiting high above the Earth.

definition: to soak completely so nothing more can be absorbed
example: The heavy rain will saturate the ground until puddles form on every surface.

definition: a possible situation or sequence of events that could happen
example: In the best-case scenario, we finish the project early and have extra time to decorate.

definition: a section or part of a larger area or system
example: The tech sector of the economy has grown rapidly in the past decade.

definition: one of the two groups in Congress that makes laws
example: Each state sends two senators to the United States Senate in Washington, D.C.

definition: keeping soldiers around a place so no one can get in or out until they give up
example: The castle withstood the siege for three months before supplies ran out.

definition: the importance or meaning of something
example: The significance of her discovery was huge—it changed how doctors treat the disease.

definition: happening at the very same moment
example: The two runners crossed the finish line in a simultaneous tie—neither one ahead of the other.

definition: having doubts about whether something is true
example: She was skeptical that the magic trick was real and looked for hidden wires.

definition: very serious and not cheerful
example: The crowd stood in solemn silence as the flag was lowered to half-mast.

definition: to some degree but not completely
example: The test was somewhat difficult—not impossible, but harder than he expected.

definition: having the highest power to rule itself, free from outside control
example: A sovereign nation makes its own laws without being controlled by another country.

definition: a complete range of something, or the band of colors in light
example: A rainbow shows the full spectrum of colors in sunlight, from red all the way to violet.

definition: to guess about something without having all the facts
example: Scientists speculate that there might be life on other planets, but we haven't found proof yet.

definition: a perfectly round shape like a ball, the same in every way you look at it
example: Earth isn't a perfect sphere—it's slightly wider at the equator than at the poles.

definition: the state of being steady and not likely to change or fall
example: The wide base gives the pyramid its stability—it has stood for over four thousand years.

definition: to walk unsteadily, or to arrange things at different times
example: After spinning around ten times, she began to stagger dizzily across the yard.

definition: something to lose or gain in a situation, or a pointed post
example: With first place at stake, both teams played their hardest in the final game.

definition: a number fact collected from studying data
example: A surprising statistic is that the average person spends six months of their life waiting at red lights.

definition: a written law passed by a government body
example: The city passed a statute requiring all buildings to have working smoke detectors.

definition: a too-simple and often unfair idea about a group of people
example: It's a stereotype that all scientists wear lab coats—many work outdoors in boots and jeans.

definition: to encourage activity or make something more active
example: Bright colors and interesting puzzles stimulate a baby's brain development.

definition: lower in rank or less important than something else
example: In a company, a junior worker is subordinate to the manager and must follow their instructions.

definition: coming after something else in time or order
example: The first earthquake was small, but the subsequent ones were much stronger.

definition: money given by the government to help keep prices low or support a trade
example: The government provides a subsidy to farmers, so they can afford to grow food at lower prices.

definition: any material you can see, touch, or measure
example: Water is the only common substance that exists as a solid, liquid, and gas on Earth.

definition: large in size, amount, or importance
example: She saved a substantial amount of money—enough to buy a brand-new bicycle.

definition: so slight that it is hard to notice unless you look carefully
example: There was a subtle difference between the two shades of blue—barely visible to most people.

definition: a person who takes over a position after someone else leaves
example: The retiring principal introduced her successor who would start leading the school next month.

definition: to briefly state the main points without extra details
example: Can you summarize the whole chapter in just three sentences?

definition: great; of the highest quality
example: The big band gave a superb performance—the audience gave them a five-minute standing ovation.

definition: highest in rank, power, or importance
example: The Supreme Court is the highest court in the United States and makes final legal decisions.

definition: more than what is needed; an extra amount
example: The farm produced a surplus of tomatoes, so they donated the extras to the food bank.

definition: to give up or stop fighting
example: He refused to surrender and kept debugging until the code finally worked at midnight.

definition: made by humans rather than found in nature; artificial
example: Nylon is a synthetic material invented in the 1930s that doesn't exist in nature.

definition: a person who pays rent to live in a building they don't own
example: The tenant on the third floor always pays rent on the first of every month.

definition: the end point, or a building where travelers begin their journey
example: We arrived at the airport terminal two hours before our flight was scheduled to leave.

definition: the physical features of a stretch of land
example: The rocky terrain made it hard for the hikers to walk without twisting an ankle.

definition: to give evidence or state facts under oath in court
example: The witness was asked to testify about exactly what he saw that night.

definition: how a surface feels when you touch it
example: The texture of sandpaper is rough and scratchy, while silk feels smooth and soft.

definition: treatment that helps heal the body or mind
example: After breaking her leg, she needed six weeks of physical therapy to walk normally again.

definition: a warning that something harmful may happen, or a source of danger
example: Climate change is a serious threat to polar bears as their ice habitat melts away.

definition: wood used for building, or trees grown for their wood
example: Loggers cut timber from the forest to make lumber for building houses.

definition: poisonous and able to cause harm or death
example: The bright colors of the poison dart frog warn predators that its skin is toxic.

definition: an exchange of goods services, or money between people
example: Every transaction at the store is recorded, so the owner knows perfectly what was sold.

definition: the process of changing from one state or condition to another
example: The transition from elementary to middle school can feel exciting and scary at the same time.

definition: to send or pass something from one place or person to another
example: Radio towers transmit signals that your car radio picks up and turns into music.

definition: something that causes an event or reaction to start
example: The loud noise was the trigger that caused all the birds to fly away at once.

definition: full of sudden violent movements or changes; chaotic
example: The flight hit turbulent air, and the plane shook so much that drinks spilled everywhere.

definition: the dim light in the sky just after sunset or before sunrise
example: Fireflies begin to glow during twilight filling the backyard with tiny floating lights.

definition: the final or most extreme; the best possible
example: Winning the championship was the ultimate achievement after four years of hard training.

definition: to slowly weaken or damage something
example: Constant criticism can undermine a person's confidence until they stop trying altogether.

definition: stubbornly refusing to change one's mind
example: The obstinate child refused to eat vegetables no matter what his parents said.

definition: to remove or prevent a problem or trouble
example: Wearing a seatbelt can obviate serious injuries during a car accident.

definition: involving a lot of effort and trouble
example: The onerous task of cleaning the entire house took the whole weekend.

definition: the quality of being hard to see through or understand
example: The opacity of the murky water made it impossible to see the bottom.

definition: great wealth and luxury
example: The opulence of the palace was shown by its golden walls and crystal chandeliers.

definition: a formal speech given on an important occasion
example: The president delivered a powerful oration at the national ceremony.

definition: regular movement back and forth
example: The oscillation of the pendulum kept perfect time on the grandfather clock.

definition: appearing to be true but possibly not the real reason
example: The ostensible reason for the trip was business, but she really just wanted a vacation.

definition: excluding someone from a group
example: The ostracism of the new kid made the teacher step in and help.

definition: a friendly action to start a bond or agreement
example: The new neighbor's overture of baking cookies helped her make friends quickly.

definition: pleasant to taste or acceptable to the mind
example: Adding honey made the bitter medicine more palatable for the child.

definition: a solution for all problems
example: Exercise is not a panacea, but it does help with many health issues.

definition: a perfect example of a good quality
example: The volunteer was a paragon of kindness who helped everyone in need.

definition: seeming to contradict itself but it could be true
example: It was paradoxical that the fire station itself caught fire.

definition: a very small amount of something
example: The paucity of rain caused the crops to dry up in the field.

definition: a teacher, especially a strict one
example: The stern pedagogue expected every student to sit quietly and pay attention.

definition: caring too much about small details and rules
example: The pedantic editor corrected each tiny grammar mistake in the letter.

definition: showing that you think poorly of someone or something
example: Calling someone lazy is a pejorative term that can hurt their feelings.

definition: the feeling of being sorry for doing something wrong
example: His penitence was clear when he apologized and offered to fix the broken window.

definition: second to last in a series
example: The penultimate chapter of the book had the most exciting plot twist.

definition: harmful in a way that is not easily noticed
example: The pernicious effects of bullying can last long after the bullying stops.

definition: a feeling of worry or unease
example: The sudden change in schedule caused perturbation among the students.

definition: being grumpy and rude like a small child
example: His petulance when he didn't get his way annoyed everyone around him.

definition: calm and peaceful with little movement
example: The placid lake reflected the mountains like a perfect mirror.

definition: a remark that has been used too often and has no meaning
example: Saying 'everything happens for a reason' felt like an empty platitude.

definition: way more than enough of something
example: The store offered a plethora of ice cream flavors to choose from.

definition: causing a sharp feeling of sadness or sympathy
example: The poignant ending of the movie made everyone in the theater cry.

definition: a strong written or spoken attack on a belief or opinion
example: The newspaper article was a polemic against the new school uniform policy.

definition: slow and heavy; dull and boring
example: The elephant's ponderous steps shook the ground as it walked by.

definition: warning that something important or bad is about to happen
example: The dark clouds were a portentous sign that a severe storm was approaching.

definition: dealing with things in a real-world, hands-on way
example: His pragmatism led him to study something useful rather than just interesting.

definition: not safe or secure; likely to fall or fail
example: The vase sat in a precarious position on the edge of the shelf.

definition: very steep or happening very quickly
example: The precipitous cliff dropped straight down to the ocean below.

definition: something that comes before and leads to something else
example: Dark clouds are often a precursor to heavy rain.

definition: a preference or special liking for something
example: Her predilection for chocolate cake meant she always ordered it for her birthday.

definition: better or more important than all others
example: She was the preeminent scientist in the field of marine biology.

definition: a greater amount or the majority of something
example: The preponderance of proof pointed to the suspect being guilty.

definition: a feeling that something bad is about to happen
example: She had a presentiment that the picnic would be ruined by rain.

definition: doing something without permission or right to do so
example: It was presumptuous of him to sit in the teacher's chair without asking.

definition: existing from the very start of time
example: Scientists study primordial rocks to learn about Earth's earliest history.

definition: complete honesty and high moral standards
example: The judge was known for his probity and always made fair decisions.

definition: a natural tendency to behave in a certain way
example: His proclivity for telling jokes made him the funniest person in the class.

definition: to delay doing something that needs to be done
example: She would always procrastinate on her homework until the night before it was due.

definition: very great in size, amount, or degree
example: The prodigious waterfall sent millions of gallons of water crashing down each hour.

definition: spending money or resources carelessly and wastefully
example: The profligate spending of the club used up all the funds in one month.

definition: to predict what will happen in the future
example: Weather forecasters try to prognosticate whether it will rain or shine.

definition: officially announcing a new law or rule
example: The promulgation of the new safety rules needed signs posted in every hallway.

definition: giving a good chance of success; favorable
example: The sunny weather was propitious for the outdoor school picnic.

definition: owned by one person or company and not shared
example: The recipe was proprietary and kept secret by the restaurant owner.

definition: to officially forbid something
example: The school rules proscribe running in the hallways for safety reasons.

definition: to try to persuade someone to join a cause or belief
example: She didn't want to proselytize, but she happily shared her love of reading.

definition: lasting longer than expected or needed
example: The protracted meeting went on for three hours instead of one.

definition: the place of origin or history of something
example: The museum checked the provenance of the painting to make sure it was authentic.

definition: temporary and likely to be changed later
example: The provisional schedule will be updated once the final details are confirmed.

definition: great skill or ability in something
example: Her athletic prowess helped her win three gold medals at the competition.

definition: careful thought before making decisions
example: Prudence told him to save some money instead of spending it all on candy.

definition: wanting to fight; quick to start arguments
example: The pugnacious rooster chased anyone who came near the chicken coop.

definition: great physical beauty
example: The pulchritude of the sunset over the ocean took everyone's breath away.

definition: very careful about details and correct behavior
example: The punctilious host made sure every plate and glass was perfectly placed.

definition: intended as a punishment
example: The punitive measures for cheating included a zero on the test.

definition: a difficult and complex situation that is hard to escape
example: The argument turned into a quagmire with no easy fix in sight.

definition: a feeling of doubt or unease about doing something
example: She had no qualm about speaking up when she saw something unfair happening.

definition: a state of confusion about what to do
example: He was in a quandary about whether to join the soccer team, or the drama club.

definition: likely to argue or disagree with people
example: The quarrelsome siblings argued about everything from toys to television shows.

definition: always whining and finding fault
example: The querulous toddler whined about every meal that was put in front of him.

definition: being the most perfect example of something
example: Apple pie is the quintessential American dessert served at many gatherings.

definition: dreamy and noble but not doable in real life
example: His quixotic dream of ending all pollution in one year was admirable but unlikely.

definition: full of energy and hard to control
example: The rambunctious puppies knocked over the water bowl while playing.

definition: bitter anger and resentment
example: After years of rancor, the two neighbors finally made peace.

definition: very greedy and always wanting more
example: The rapacious pirate searched every ship for treasure and gold.

definition: a return to friendly relations between two groups
example: The rapprochement between the two rival schools led to a joint charity event.

definition: the reasons or thinking behind a choice
example: The teacher explained the rationale for assigning the hard project.

definition: to summarize the main points of something
example: At the end of the lesson, the teacher asked a student to recapitulate the important points.

definition: the tendency to repeat bad behavior after being punished
example: Programs that teach job skills help reduce recidivism among young offenders.

definition: trading favors so both sides gain
example: The two friends practiced reciprocity by taking turns helping each other study.

definition: the process of returning land or materials to a useful state
example: The reclamation of the old factory site turned it into a beautiful community park.

definition: the restoring of friendly relations after a fight
example: The reconciliation between the two friends happened when they talked things out.

definition: a way of correcting something that is wrong or unfair
example: The student sought redress after being unfairly punished for something she didn't do.

definition: the state of being no longer needed or useful
example: The redundancy of two identical paragraphs made the essay feel repetitive.

definition: to repair and make something look new again
example: The workers helped refurbish the old library with fresh paint and new furniture.

definition: to say or do something again for emphasis
example: The loop will reiterate the same steps until the counter reaches one hundred.

definition: to put someone or something in a less important position
example: The old toys were relegated to the attic when new ones arrived.

definition: to protest or complain strongly about something
example: The parents remonstrated against the choice to cancel the school play.

definition: formally giving up a right or something you own
example: Her renunciation of the prize showed her humble character.

definition: something done to make up for a wrong
example: The student offered reparation by replacing the book he had accidentally damaged.

definition: so bad that it deserves blame
example: Bullying is reprehensible behavior that should never be tolerated in school.

definition: the rejection of something as having no authority or truth
example: The scientist's repudiation of the false theory led to new research.

definition: an official request or demand for something
example: The teacher submitted a requisition for new art supplies for the classroom.

definition: to officially cancel a law order, or choice
example: The principal chose to rescind the new rule after students complained.

definition: a calm and sometimes sad acceptance of something unpleasant you cannot change
example: With a sigh of resignation, he accepted that the picnic was canceled due to rain.

definition: very determined and unwilling to give up
example: The resolute climber kept on up the mountain despite the freezing wind.

definition: rising again after a period of decline
example: The resurgent team won five games in a row after losing their first three.

definition: being unwilling to speak or reveal feelings
example: His reticence about the surprise party kept everyone from finding out.

definition: looking back at things that happened in the past
example: The museum held a retrospective exhibit showing the artist's work over many years.

definition: noisy and lively party
example: The New Year's Eve revelry included music dancing, and fireworks.

definition: to echo; to have lasting effects
example: The thunder reverberated through the valley and shook the cabin windows.

definition: a long and fussy process with too many steps
example: Getting a library card involved so much rigmarole with forms and signatures.

definition: to think deeply and carefully about something
example: She liked to ruminate on big questions while walking in the park alone.

definition: too important to be changed or interfered with
example: Family dinner time was sacrosanct and no one was allowed to miss it.

definition: wise and good at making smart choices
example: The sagacious grandmother always gave the best advice to her grandchildren.

definition: most important or noticeable
example: The salient point of the presentation was that recycling saves energy.

definition: optimistic and positive, especially in difficult situations
example: Despite the rain, she remained sanguine that the outdoor party would still be fun.

definition: mocking or scornful in a humorous way
example: His sardonic comment about the cold weather made everyone laugh.

definition: very careful and thorough about details
example: The scrupulous accountant checked each number twice to avoid errors.

definition: to examine something carefully
example: The detective scrutinized every inch of the room looking for clues.

definition: spending a lot of time sitting and not being active
example: A sedentary lifestyle can lead to health problems if you don't exercise.

definition: an appearance or outward form of something
example: The classroom returned to a semblance of order after the fire drill ended.

definition: finding something good by accident or luck
example: Finding a twenty-dollar bill on the sidewalk was pure serendipity.

definition: too willing to serve or please others
example: The servile assistant agreed with everything the boss said without thinking.

definition: the quality of two or more events taking place at once
example: The simultaneity of the two fireworks made the sky light up in a spectacular burst.

definition: comfort during a time of sadness or letdown
example: Reading books gave him solace when he was feeling lonely.

definition: showing care and concern for someone
example: The solicitous nurse checked on every patient many times during the night.

definition: making you feel sleepy
example: The soporific hum of the car engine put the baby to sleep in minutes.

definition: false or not genuine
example: The spurious claim that the earth is flat has been proven wrong many times.

definition: very dirty and unpleasant
example: The squalid apartment had broken windows and trash piled in every corner.

definition: a state of staying still with no growth or change
example: Economic stagnation meant that few new jobs were created in the town.

definition: a fixed regular payment, usually small
example: The intern received a small stipend to cover food and transportation costs.

definition: very strict without room to bend
example: The stringent safety rules required all workers to wear helmets at all times.

definition: bringing someone under control by force
example: The subjugation of the native people by colonizers lasted for many decades.

definition: below the level of conscious awareness
example: The subliminal message in the advertisement was too quick for viewers to notice.

definition: placing something in a less important position
example: The subordination of fun to homework was a hard lesson for the child to learn.

definition: willing to do whatever someone else wants
example: The robot was designed to be subservient and follow every command it received.

definition: evidence that proves something is true
example: The scientist needed substantiation of her theory before publishing the results.

definition: a trick or deception used to achieve a goal
example: The students used subterfuge to plan the surprise party without the teacher knowing.

definition: expressed clearly in just a few words
example: Good code comments are succinct—just enough words to explain what the line does.

definition: a close bond between two different organisms that benefits both
example: The symbiosis between bees and flowers helps both species survive and thrive.

definition: only slightly related to the main topic
example: His tangential comments about sports during the science lesson confused the class.

definition: equal to something in its effect or value
example: Not voting is tantamount to giving up your voice in the community.

definition: boldness or nerve that might be considered rude
example: He had the temerity to tell the chef that the food was terrible.

definition: holding back and not going too far in what you do
example: Temperance in eating sweets helped her keep her teeth healthy.

definition: a strong will to keep going and never give up
example: Her tenacity in practicing piano every day led to her winning the competition.

definition: a principle or belief held by a group
example: One tenet of the science club was that every thought deserved to be tested.

definition: barely warm, or showing little interest
example: The audience gave a tepid response to the boring presentation.

definition: using very few words in a way that seems rude
example: His terse reply of 'no' left no room for further discussion.

definition: a long angry speech of criticism
example: The coach went on a tirade about the team's lack of effort during practice.

definition: a state of not being active or energetic
example: The hot summer afternoon left the entire class in a state of torpor.

definition: easy to control or deal with
example: The tractable puppy quickly learned to sit and shake hands on command.

definition: lasting only for a short time
example: The rainbow was transient and disappeared within just a few minutes.

definition: a feeling of fear about something that might happen
example: He felt trepidation before his first day at a brand-new school.

definition: fierce and ready to start a fight at any moment
example: The truculent boy picked arguments with everyone on the playground.

definition: the state of being everywhere at the same time
example: The ubiquity of smartphones means almost everyone has one in their pocket.

definition: a feeling of being offended by something
example: She took umbrage at the rude comment and walked away from the conversation.

definition: so full of fake praise that it feels slimy and dishonest
example: The unctuous salesman smiled too much and made the customers uncomfortable.

definition: the foundation or basic support for something
example: Hard work is the underpinning of success in any field.

definition: leaving no doubt; completely clear
example: The coach's unequivocal 'no' meant there was no chance of skipping practice.

definition: willing to do dishonest things to get what one wants
example: The unscrupulous merchant sold fake jewelry as if it was real gold.

definition: seizing power that doesn't belong to you
example: The general's usurpation of the throne shocked the entire kingdom.

definition: designed to be useful rather than attractive
example: The utilitarian backpack was not pretty but had many pockets for organizing supplies.

definition: to keep changing your mind about something
example: She would vacillate between chocolate and vanilla every time she bought ice cream.

definition: dull and lacking any spark
example: The vapid television show was so boring that everyone changed the channel.

definition: great force or passion in expressing feelings
example: He argued with such vehemence that everyone in the room fell silent.

definition: to regard with great respect and admiration
example: The town venerated the elderly teacher who had taught three generations.

definition: telling the truth; honest and accurate
example: The veracious reporter always double-checked her facts before publishing a story.

definition: using more words than needed
example: His verbose essay was ten pages long when two pages would have been enough.

definition: a small remaining part of something that once existed
example: The crumbling wall was the last vestige of the ancient castle.

definition: saying cruel, untrue things to ruin someone's name
example: The vilification of the honest politician was based on lies and rumors.

definition: wanting revenge and trying to hurt someone
example: The vindictive bully spread rumors about the student who reported him.

definition: rare and great skill in music or another artistic pursuit
example: The violinist's virtuosity left the crowd amazed by every single note.

definition: filled with bitter criticism or hate
example: The vitriolic review of the restaurant made the chef feel awful.

definition: loud and forceful in expressing opinions
example: The vociferous crowd chanted slogans during the peaceful protest march.

definition: the power of choosing or making decisions
example: He joined the club of his own volition, not because anyone forced him.

definition: an official paper giving up a right or claim
example: Parents signed a waiver before students could go on the field trip.

definition: done on purpose and without caring about harm caused
example: The wanton destruction of the garden upset the entire neighborhood.

definition: justified or deserved based on the situation
example: The teacher's concern was warranted the student had missed many classes.

definition: the money or resources needed to do something
example: The family lacked the wherewithal to repair the roof after the storm.

definition: a person with extreme enthusiasm for a cause
example: The environmental zealot spent every weekend cleaning up local beaches.

definition: choosing to give up something important to you
example: Her abnegation of her own time showed her dedication to the team.

definition: sharp and direct in speech, often in a harsh way
example: His acerbic wit made people laugh but sometimes hurt their feelings.

definition: something that belongs to a different time period
example: A knight in shining armor at a modern office would be an anachronism.

definition: something that a person hates and rejects with all their heart
example: Cheating on tests was anathema to the honest student.

definition: the exact opposite of something
example: War is the antithesis of peace in every possible way.

definition: harshness or sharpness of tone or manner
example: She spoke with such asperity that the room went completely silent.

definition: a false statement made to damage someone's reputation
example: The calumny spread about the teacher was completely untrue and harmful.

definition: the use of dishonest tricks to deceive people
example: The dishonest salesman used chicanery to trick customers into paying too much.

definition: stubbornly refusing to obey
example: The contumacious cat ignored each command and sat wherever it pleased.

definition: to commit to doing a task or project
example: The class chose to undertake the challenge of cleaning up the entire beach in one day.

definition: to open up gradually, or to come to light
example: The mystery began to unfold as the detective found new clues at every turn.

definition: applying to everyone and all things
example: Laughter is a universal language—people everywhere understand a smile.

definition: plant life in general, especially in a certain area
example: Thick green vegetation covered the jungle floor making it hard to walk through.

definition: to check whether something is true or accurate
example: Always verify facts from the internet by checking at least two different sources.

definition: against; used to show two sides competing
example: Today's big game is the Eagles versus the Hawks—the two best teams in the league.

definition: the power to reject or block a decision
example: The president used the veto to block the law from going into effect.

definition: by way of; going through a particular place or method
example: We traveled from New York to Los Angeles via Chicago, stopping for lunch there.

definition: to shake rapidly back and forth with small movements
example: Guitar strings vibrate when you pluck them, creating sound waves you hear as music.

definition: likely to change suddenly and unpredictably
example: The weather in spring is volatile—sunny one hour and pouring rain the next.

definition: money paid to a worker for the hours they work
example: The minimum wage is the lowest amount an employer is legally allowed to pay per hour.

definition: to be strong enough to survive something without being damaged
example: The fortress was built to withstand attacks, with walls ten feet thick.

definition: existing as an idea rather than something physical you can touch
example: Love and justice are abstract concepts—you can't hold them, but you can feel their effects.

definition: a school or institution for special training
example: She attended a dance academy where students practiced ballet for six hours each day.

definition: the ability or right to enter, use, or approach something
example: A library card gives you access to thousands of books, movies, and computers.

definition: next to or near something without something in between
example: The playground is adjacent to the school building—just a few steps from the back door.

definition: to link up with a bigger group
example: The local soccer club chose to affiliate with the national league to compete in bigger tournaments.

definition: a list of topics to discuss or things to do at a meeting
example: The first item on today's meeting agenda is planning the spring concert.

definition: a total made by adding parts together
example: The aggregate score from all three rounds determined who won the competition.

definition: to stir up or make someone feel worried and upset
example: Loud noises tend to agitate the horses, making them stomp and neigh nervously.

definition: a step-by-step set of instructions for solving a problem
example: The computer uses an algorithm to sort thousands of photos by date in just seconds.

definition: loyalty and support given to a country, leader, or cause
example: Citizens say the Pledge of Allegiance to show loyalty to their country.

definition: to give a share of something for a specific purpose
example: The teacher allotted fifteen minutes for each group to present their science project.

definition: a country or person who agrees to help and support another
example: France was an important ally of the American colonies during the Revolutionary War.

definition: the height of something above sea level
example: At high altitude in the mountains, the air has less oxygen and breathing feels harder.

definition: a heavy object that keeps a boat from drifting, or something that holds firm
example: The ship dropped its heavy iron anchor to stay in place during the storm.

definition: a short amusing story about a real event or person
example: Grandpa shared a funny anecdote about the time he accidentally wore two different shoes to work.

definition: with the name unknown or not revealed
example: Someone left an anonymous note in the idea box asking for more recess time.

definition: based on random choice rather than any reason or system
example: The rule that you can't wear hats indoors seems completely arbitrary—what harm does a hat do?

definition: a set of historical documents or records stored for reference
example: The city archive contains newspapers and photographs from over a century ago.

definition: able to speak clearly and express thoughts well
example: The ten-year-old was so articulate that adults were amazed by how clearly she explained her science project.

definition: one part or feature of a larger thing
example: The most challenging aspect of the project was building the model volcano.

definition: a violent physical attack on someone
example: The castle walls protected the people inside from assault by invading armies.

definition: a group of people gathered together for a purpose
example: The entire school met in the gymnasium for the Friday morning assembly.

definition: to reach or achieve something you've been working toward
example: He studied every day to attain his goal of earning straight A's this semester.

definition: having the freedom to govern itself or make its own decisions
example: An autonomous robot can vacuum the house without anyone controlling it.

definition: the scenery behind something or the setting for events
example: Snow-covered mountains gave a stunning backdrop for the holiday photograph.

definition: too dry or poor to produce plants; empty of life
example: The barren desert stretched for miles without a single tree or blade of grass.

definition: a large group of soldiers organized as a unit
example: The battalion of five hundred soldiers marched across the field in perfect rows.

definition: a standard point of reference used to measure performance
example: Scoring eighty percent on the test was the benchmark for passing to the next level.

definition: a person who gets the benefit
example: Students are the main beneficiaries of the new library because they get access to more books.

definition: to support or strengthen something
example: Reading reviews from happy customers helped bolster confidence in the new restaurant.

definition: a break or gap in something, especially a wall or agreement
example: Water poured through the breach in the dam and flooded the valley below.

definition: a small printed booklet with facts about something
example: The travel brochure showed stunning pictures of beaches and hotels in Hawaii.

definition: a system with many complex rules and official procedures
example: The long paperwork and waiting times showed just how slow the bureaucracy could be.

definition: to adjust an instrument to ensure it gives accurate readings
example: You must calibrate the thermometer so it shows the correct temperature.

definition: to attract and hold someone's focus fully
example: The storyteller's voice captivated the children so much that no one moved for an hour.

definition: something that causes an important change to happen faster
example: The discovery of gold was the catalyst that sent thousands of people rushing west in 1849.

definition: a sudden awful disaster causing great damage
example: The earthquake was a catastrophe that destroyed hundreds of buildings in just thirty seconds.

definition: to remove parts of something considered wrong or harmful
example: Some countries censor the news, so people cannot read about certain events.

definition: a top leader of a state or school
example: The chancellor of the school announced free tuition for students from low-income families.

definition: a written record of historical events in time order
example: The explorer kept a detailed chronicle of each island he visited during his three-year voyage.

definition: a group of people or groups joining together for a common purpose
example: Several environmental groups formed a coalition to fight against ocean pollution.

definition: about thinking, learning, and understanding
example: Playing chess builds cognitive skills like memory, planning, and problem-solving.

definition: logical and consistent; making sense as a whole
example: Her essay was coherent—each paragraph connected smoothly to the next.

definition: to work together with others on a project
example: The two artists chose to collaborate on a mural that covered the entire school wall.

definition: to honor the memory of an important person or event
example: The bronze statue was built to commemorate the soldiers who fought in the war.

definition: a raw material or basic good that can be bought and sold
example: Coffee is one of the most traded commodities in the world grown in over fifty countries.

definition: able to work well together without problems
example: The new game is compatible with both old and new versions of the gaming console.

definition: to hide something from sight or keep it secret
example: The squirrel tried to conceal its acorns by burying them under the fallen leaves.

definition: to admit that something is true, or to give up in a contest
example: After counting the votes twice, the losing candidate finally conceded the election.

definition: a group of states or groups joined together for mutual support
example: The thirteen colonies formed a confederation to fight for being free together.

definition: a group of people gathered together for worship or a meeting
example: The entire congregation stood and sang together as the organ played.

definition: following one after another in order
example: She won the spelling bee three consecutive years in a row—third grade, fourth, and fifth.

definition: a state official who lives in a foreign city to help their country's citizens
example: When she lost her passport abroad, the consul at the embassy helped her get a new one.

definition: about one of Earth's large landmasses
example: The continental United States does not include Hawaii or Alaska.

definition: depending on something else happening first
example: The picnic is contingent on good weather—if it rains, we'll eat inside instead.

definition: the point where two or more things come together
example: The convergence of the two rivers created a wide, strong waterway.

definition: a connection or link between two things
example: Scientists found a correlation between exercise and better sleep quality.

definition: the quality of being trusted and believed
example: Using facts from real scientists gives your report more credibility.

definition: a point where two roads meet, or a time of important decision
example: The town stood at a crossroads and had to decide between building a mall or keeping the park.

definition: white puffy clouds that look like cotton balls in the sky
example: The cumulus clouds floating overhead looked like a parade of giant marshmallows.

definition: a person who takes care of art and items in a museum
example: The curator carefully arranged the dinosaur bones, so visitors could see the complete bones.

definition: a solid shape with circular ends and straight sides, like a can
example: A soup can is shaped like a cylinder—round on the top and bottom with straight sides.

definition: about how many people live somewhere and who they are
example: The demographic data showed that the town's population had doubled in ten years.

definition: to publicly declare something as wrong or evil
example: Leaders around the world denounced the attack and called for immediate peace.

definition: to move something into position for use
example: The fire department chose to deploy extra trucks to the area during the dry season.

definition: a place where goods are stored or vehicles are kept
example: The bus depot holds fifty buses overnight and sends them out on routes each morning.

definition: a person who acts as a substitute or assistant to a leader
example: When the sheriff was away, the deputy was in charge of keeping the town safe.

definition: a person who comes from a specific ancestor
example: He is a descendant of one of the first families who settled in the town in 1820.

definition: to keep someone from leaving; to hold them in a place
example: Airport security had to detain the traveler because his luggage triggered the alarm.

definition: to discourage someone from doing something by making them afraid of consequences
example: The tall fence was built to deter raccoons from raiding the garbage cans at night.

definition: to know or show the differences between things
example: Twins can be hard to differentiate unless you know them well enough to spot tiny differences.

definition: showing careful and persistent effort in work
example: The diligent student checked her homework three times before turning it in.

definition: the skill of managing relations between countries peacefully
example: Through diplomacy rather than fighting, the two nations resolved their border dispute.

definition: very serious or urgent
example: The animals were in dire need of water after the creek dried up during the drought.

definition: written or spoken talk about a serious topic
example: The classroom discourse about climate change involved each student sharing research findings.

definition: the freedom to decide what should be done; also, being careful not to share secrets
example: Use your discretion when choosing partners—pick someone who works well with you.

definition: to spread out or scatter in different directions
example: When the bell rang, the crowd began to disperse and students walked to their classrooms.

definition: a person's natural tendency in behavior or mood
example: The golden retriever has a friendly disposition and wags its tail at everyone.

definition: to interrupt and prevent something from continuing normally
example: The fire alarm will disrupt class, but safety drills are important practice.

definition: disagreement with an official view or decision
example: Two judges expressed dissent, disagreeing with the court's final decision.

definition: a set of beliefs or rules taught by a group or government
example: The Monroe Doctrine stated that European countries should not interfere in the Americas.

definition: a place where someone lives; a home
example: The cliff dwellings were homes carved into rock faces by ancient Pueblo people.

definition: a line of rulers from the same family
example: The Ming dynasty ruled China for nearly three hundred years and built the Forbidden City.

definition: a conductor through which electric current enters or leaves a device
example: The battery has two electrodes—one positive and one negative—that allow current to flow.

definition: able to express thoughts clearly and persuasively
example: The student gave an eloquent speech that moved the entire audience to tears.

definition: the building where a country's representatives work in a foreign nation
example: If you lose your passport while traveling, visit your country's embassy for help.

definition: to include or contain a wide range of things
example: The national park encompasses forests, rivers, mountains, and meadows across five hundred square miles.

definition: to publicly support or suggest something
example: The famous athlete agreed to endorse the new running shoe by wearing it in every race.

definition: to imagine or picture something in your mind, especially the future
example: She could envision the empty lot transformed into a thriving community garden.

definition: a disease that spreads quickly to many people in an area
example: The flu epidemic kept so many students home that the school closed for a week.

definition: a state of balance where opposing forces are equal
example: A seesaw reaches equilibrium when both children weigh the same, and it stays level.

definition: to become more serious or intense step by step
example: A small argument can quickly escalate into a big fight if no one stays calm.

definition: the practice of spying to obtain secret information
example: The spy movie was full of espionage with agents using hidden cameras and secret codes.

definition: about a group who share culture, language, and traditions
example: The festival celebrated ethnic diversity with food, music, and dances from around the world.

definition: to leave a dangerous place quickly for safety
example: Firefighters told everyone to evacuate the building immediately when smoke filled the hallway.

definition: to use strength or effort to do something
example: You have to exert a lot of force to push open the heavy iron gate.

definition: being forced to leave your home country as punishment
example: The king was sent into exile and spent the rest of his life in a foreign country.

definition: the amount of money spent on something
example: The school's biggest expenditure this year was buying new laptops for every classroom.

definition: a small group within a larger one that disagrees with the majority
example: A faction of students wanted pizza while the rest of the class voted for tacos.

definition: irregular rising and falling; unpredictable change
example: The fluctuation in temperature from hot to cold made it hard to decide what to wear.

definition: to lose something as a penalty, or give it up unwillingly
example: If your team doesn't show up, you forfeit the game, and the other team wins automatically.

definition: inspiring fear or respect because of being impressively large or capable
example: The opposing center was formidable—six feet tall and blocking every shot.

definition: to make stronger or more secure against attack
example: The soldiers used sandbags to fortify the walls against the rising floodwater.

definition: the crime of tricking people to gain money or advantage dishonestly
example: The scammer committed fraud by selling fake concert tickets that didn't actually exist.

definition: careful with money; avoiding waste
example: My frugal grandmother reuses wrapping paper and never throws away leftovers.

definition: a group of soldiers stationed at a fort to protect it
example: The garrison of two hundred soldiers guarded the fort day and night.

definition: a newspaper or official journal
example: The town gazette published a story about the record-breaking pumpkin grown by a local farmer.

definition: a scientist who studies rocks, minerals, and Earth's structure
example: The geologist examined the layers of rock in the cliff to find out how old they were.

definition: a narrow valley with steep rocky walls, often with a river at the bottom
example: The river had carved a deep gorge through the rock over millions of years.

definition: the rate at which something changes, especially heat or slope
example: The steep gradient of the hill made cyclists shift to their lowest gear.

definition: to struggle or wrestle with something hard
example: Students grapple with long division before it finally clicks and becomes easy.

definition: a large area of land covered especially with grass rather than trees
example: The African grasslands are home to zebras, lions, and huge herds of wildebeest.

definition: a formal complaint about something believed to be wrong or unfair
example: The workers filed a grievance because they were not being paid for overtime hours.

definition: the total amount before something is subtracted, or disgusting
example: The company's gross income was one million dollars before paying taxes and expenses.

definition: concerned with helping people who are suffering
example: The humanitarian group sent food, water, and medicine to earthquake survivors.

definition: to catch fire or cause something to start burning
example: A single spark from the campfire can ignite dry leaves and start a forest fire.

definition: to charge a public official with serious wrongdoing while in office
example: Congress has the power to impeach a president who breaks the law while serving.

definition: about an empire or ruled by an emperor
example: The imperial palace in Beijing was home to Chinese emperors for five hundred years.

definition: how often something occurs in a certain group or area
example: The incidence of cavities dropped after the school started a daily tooth-brushing program.

definition: by nature belonging to a place; native
example: The kangaroo is indigenous to Australia and is not found wild anywhere else in the world.

definition: the basic systems a society needs to function, like roads and power
example: Good infrastructure like smooth roads and reliable power helps a city grow.

definition: to put liquid into something using a needle or force
example: The nurse will inject the vaccine into your arm with a tiny needle that barely stings.

definition: born with a quality; not learned through experience
example: Some people seem to have an innate talent for music—they can play by ear without lessons.

definition: to bring in new ideas or methods that improve things
example: The company constantly tries to innovate by making products no one has thought of before.

definition: to give a vaccine to protect against disease
example: Doctors inoculate babies against risky diseases in their first year of life.

definition: ideas or info contributed to a project, or data entered into a computer
example: The teacher asked for input from every student when deciding on the field trip destination.

definition: words carved or written on a surface as a permanent record
example: The inscription on the old tombstone was so worn that only a few letters remained readable.

definition: too small or unimportant to be worth considering
example: The tiny scratch on the car was so insignificant that nobody even noticed it.

definition: whole and undamaged; not broken
example: Amazingly, the glass vase survived the earthquake and remained completely intact.

definition: short-term; serving during the time between two events
example: An interim principal led the school for two months while they searched for a permanent one.

definition: a place where two roads or lines cross each other
example: Always look both ways at an intersection before crossing the street.

definition: very close and personal, or private
example: He shared the intimate details of his childhood with only his closest friends.

definition: fearless and adventurous
example: The intrepid explorer ventured into the unknown jungle where no human had gone before.

definition: belonging to something as a basic and essential part
example: Curiosity is an intrinsic part of being a scientist—they always want to know why.

definition: a complete list of items in stock or owned
example: The store clerk counted every item to make sure the inventory matched the computer records.

definition: not based on reason or logic
example: His fear of butterflies was completely irrational—they cannot harm anyone.

definition: a small island
example: The tiny isle in the middle of the lake had just one palm tree, and a sandy beach.

definition: special words used by a specific profession that outsiders find confusing
example: The doctor used so much medical jargon that the patient had to ask what every word meant.

definition: about courts, judges, and legal decisions
example: The judicial branch of government includes all the courts and judges in the country.

definition: the official power to make legal decisions in a certain area
example: The city police have jurisdiction within town limits, but the sheriff covers the county.

definition: young and not fully grown, or acting childish
example: The juvenile eagle still had brown feathers and wouldn't grow its white head for two more years.

definition: about movement and the energy it creates
example: A rolling bowling ball has kinetic energy that transfers to the pins when it hits them.

definition: a brief failure or slip, or a period of time passing
example: After a lapse of ten seconds, she remembered the answer and shouted it out.

definition: laws that have been made by a government
example: New legislation requires all restaurants to list the calories in each menu item.

definition: a person who helps two groups talk and work together
example: The parent liaison between the school and families helped translate newsletters into five languages.

definition: producing a lot of profit or money
example: Selling homemade cookies at the fair turned out to be quite lucrative—she earned eighty dollars.

definition: a judge who handles minor legal cases
example: The magistrate settled the small dispute between neighbors over a noisy dog.

definition: a handwritten or typed paper, especially an author's first text
example: The author mailed her finished manuscript to the publisher and waited nervously for a reply.

definition: about the sea, ships, and sailors
example: The maritime museum displays old ships, navigation tools, and maps used by ancient sailors.

definition: to follow a winding path without hurrying
example: The river meanders through the valley, curving back and forth like a lazy snake.

definition: to help two sides reach an agreement by talking to both
example: The school counselor offered to mediate between the two friends who were fighting.

definition: a book about someone's own memories and feelings
example: The astronaut wrote a memoir describing what it felt like to see Earth from space.

definition: goods that are bought and sold in stores
example: The gift shop sold all kinds of merchandise with the zoo's logo: t-shirts, mugs, and stuffed animals.

definition: about a big city and the towns around it
example: The metropolitan area includes the main city plus all the suburbs surrounding it.

definition: willing to use strong or aggressive action for a cause
example: The militant protesters blocked the road and refused to move until their demands were met.

definition: a group of normal citizens trained as soldiers for emergencies
example: During the Revolutionary War, local militia members grabbed their rifles and joined the fight.

definition: a period of one thousand years
example: We entered a new millennium when the year changed from 1999 to 2000.

definition: to organize people or resources for action
example: Volunteers mobilized quickly to fill sandbags when the river began rising toward town.

definition: a form of government ruled by a king or queen
example: England has a constitutional monarchy where the king reigns but Parliament makes the laws.

definition: about money, coins, and currency
example: The monetary value of the old coin was five hundred dollars because it was so rare.

definition: to have complete control over something allowing no one else a turn
example: Please don't monopolize the swing—let your brother have a turn after five minutes.

definition: the confidence and enthusiasm of a person or group
example: Winning three games in a row boosted the team's morale and made them believe they could win the championship.

definition: a short-term ban on doing something
example: The city placed a moratorium on new building until they could fix the overcrowded roads.

definition: a loan from a bank to buy a house, paid back over many years
example: Most families take out a mortgage to buy a home because houses cost hundreds of thousands of dollars.

definition: a rebellion by soldiers or sailors against their leaders
example: The crew staged a mutiny against the cruel captain and took control of the ship.

definition: to tell a story or describe events as they happen
example: The nature documentary was narrated by a voice that described each animal's behavior.

definition: strong pride and loyalty toward one's own country
example: Nationalism grew stronger as citizens united to compete in the Olympic Games.

definition: a specialized role or position that something fills perfectly
example: The woodpecker fills a niche in the ecosystem by eating insects hiding under bark.

definition: a system of names used in a particular field of study
example: In scientific nomenclature, humans are called Homo sapiens.

definition: moving from place to place instead of settling permanently
example: Nomadic tribes followed herds of animals across the plains for thousands of years.

definition: not helping any specific political party
example: The nonpartisan group invited speakers from each party to share their views equally.

definition: famous for something bad
example: The notorious pirate Blackbeard terrified sailors along the entire Atlantic coast.

definition: a person who is new to an activity and still learning
example: As a novice skier, she stuck to the easy slopes and fell down many times.

definition: to make something legally invalid or without effect
example: The judge chose to nullify the unfair contract because one side was tricked into signing.

definition: not well known, or difficult to understand
example: The answer was hidden in an obscure paragraph at the very bottom of page fifty-three.

definition: to block a path or prevent progress
example: The fallen tree obstructed the road, so cars had to turn around and find another way.

definition: to balance or cancel out the effect of something else
example: She hoped the extra credit assignment would offset the low grade she got on the quiz.

definition: to leave something out either on purpose or by accident
example: Be careful not to omit any steps when explaining how the test works.

definition: the start of something, especially something unpleasant
example: The onset of winter brought freezing temperatures, and the first snowfall of the year.

definition: unable to be seen through; not transparent
example: The opaque curtains blocked all sunlight, making the bedroom completely dark.

definition: to make something as good or effective as possible
example: The coach helped players optimize their running form to shave seconds off their times.

definition: a law or rule made by a city government
example: The new ordinance says all dogs must be on a leash when walking on city sidewalks.

definition: to move back and forth in a regular rhythm
example: The pendulum in a grandfather clock oscillates back and forth once every second.

definition: a sudden burst of something bad, like a disease
example: The outbreak of chicken pox affected fifteen students in just one week.

definition: the outer edges of a town or city
example: The new shopping center was built on the outskirts of town where there was more open land.

definition: to take apart and fix something fully
example: The mechanic needed to overhaul the entire engine by taking it apart and replacing worn pieces.

definition: a mistake from not noticing, or the job of watching over
example: Forgetting to invite Jake to the party was a complete oversight—we didn't mean to leave him out.

definition: done openly without any attempt to hide
example: His overt enthusiasm was obvious—he jumped up and cheered before the teacher even finished speaking.

definition: a typical example or pattern that serves as a model
example: The smartphone created a new paradigm for how people talk and access facts.

definition: a statement that seems contradictory but may really be true
example: It's a paradox that standing still in a long line can feel longer than walking a mile.

definition: the group of elected people who make laws in some countries
example: The British Parliament meets in the Palace of Westminster in London.

definition: strongly supporting one side in a conflict or political party
example: The partisan debate got heated as each side refused to think of the other's point of view.

definition: land or values inherited from one's father or ancestors
example: The ancient castle was part of the family's patrimony passed down for twelve generations.

definition: the support given by patrons or customers of a business
example: The small bookstore survived thanks to the patronage of loyal neighborhood readers.

definition: the method and practice of teaching
example: Her pedagogy included hands-on experiments because she believed students learn by doing.

definition: about punishing people who break the law
example: The penal code lists all crimes and their punishments in the state.

definition: for each person; split equally among all people
example: The per capita income shows how much money each person would get if all earnings were shared equally.

definition: lasting a very long time, or a plant that lives more than two years
example: Perennial flowers come back each spring without needing to be replanted.

definition: the outer edge or boundary of something
example: The shy fox watched from the periphery of the clearing never stepping into the open.

definition: allowing liquids or gases to pass through
example: Sandy soil is permeable, letting rain soak through quickly to the groundwater below.

definition: never ending or changing; lasting for all time
example: The perpetual motion of ocean waves never stops, crashing on the beach day and night.

definition: to treat someone cruelly because of their beliefs or identity
example: Throughout history, many groups have been persecuted for practicing their faith.

definition: the image or personality someone shows to the world
example: On stage, the comedian takes on a goofy persona that's very different from his real personality.

definition: relevant and directly related to the matter being discussed
example: The detective asked only pertinent questions that would help solve the case.

definition: spreading all through and present everywhere
example: The pervasive smell of popcorn filled every hallway as we walked toward the theater.

definition: crude oil found underground, used to make fuel and plastics
example: Petroleum is pumped from deep underground and refined into gasoline for cars.

definition: the practice of giving money to help others improve society
example: Her philanthropy funded the building of a children's hospital that treats patients for free.

definition: very important because other things depend on it
example: The invention of the printing press was a pivotal moment that changed how knowledge spread.

definition: seeming reasonable and possibly true
example: His excuse about the bus being late was plausible since it had been snowing all morning.

definition: the largest number of votes when no one gets more than half
example: She won with a plurality—more votes than anyone else but less than fifty percent of the total.

definition: all future generations of people
example: We must protect the rainforest for posterity, so our grandchildren can enjoy its wonders.

definition: dealing with things in a practical rather than idealistic way
example: The pragmatic fix was to fix the leak with tape today and call a plumber the next day.

definition: to come before something else in time or order
example: Thunder always follows lightning—the flash precedes the boom by several seconds.

definition: an earlier event or choice used as an example for future actions
example: The court's ruling set a precedent that schools must provide equal resources to all students.

definition: most common, strong, or noticeable
example: Blue was the predominant color in the painting, covering almost the entire canvas.

definition: an introduction written at the start of a book by the author
example: In the preface, the author explains why she spent five years researching dinosaurs.

definition: the process of obtaining supplies or equipment
example: The school's procurement department ordered five hundred new desks for the renovation.

definition: a young person with amazing talent or skill
example: The chess prodigy defeated adult grandmasters when he was only nine years old.

definition: skilled and competent at doing something
example: After two years of practice, she became proficient at playing the violin.

definition: to increase rapidly in number or spread widely
example: Wildflowers proliferate in the meadow each spring, turning the field into a carpet of color.

definition: information spread to influence people's opinions, often in a misleading way
example: During the war, propaganda posters encouraged citizens to support the troops.

definition: to officially charge someone with a crime in court
example: The district attorney chose to prosecute the company for dumping chemicals in the river.

definition: a document giving facts about a school, company, or investment
example: The college sent a prospectus showing campus photos, courses offered, and student activities.

definition: the official rules for how things should be done
example: Safety protocol requires all visitors to sign in at the front desk before entering the building.

definition: a large section of a country with its own local government
example: Canada is divided into ten provinces, each with its own premier and legislature.
